response:
Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day. A leap year has 366 days, so she washes her hair [number_of_times] times during that period. To find out how much shampoo she needs, multiply the number of washes by 1/4 ounce. This gives the total amount of shampoo required, which is [total_shampoo_needed] ounces. Since shampoo is sold in 14-ounce bottles, the number of bottles she needs can be determined by dividing the total amount needed by 14.

To calculate the exact number of bottles, first determine how many times she washes her hair in a leap year. Divide 366 by 2, which gives [number_of_times]. Multiply that by 1/4 to find [total_shampoo_needed]. If the result is not a whole number, round up to the nearest whole number since she cannot purchase a fraction of a bottle. This final number will tell Brittany how many 14-ounce bottles she needs to buy to last the entire year.
